# oms-exec — LLM Reference

C-suite strategic session. Selects the next milestone, plans its FEATURE blocks, queues them for elaboration. Output is product direction — never implementation.

## Triggers — use /oms-exec when

- Queue is empty and no milestone has active coverage
- A milestone completes and the next one needs to be selected
- CEO explicitly wants to review product direction
- Auto-triggered by Router when queue empties and no task is provided

Skip /oms-exec when:
- Tasks are already queued and active
- CEO has a specific task to run through /oms discussion

## Invocation

```
/oms-exec                        # CPO auto-selects highest-priority uncovered milestone
/oms-exec [milestone name]       # force a specific milestone
/oms exec                        # alias via /oms skill
```

## What it does

1. Reads product-direction.ctx.md + cleared-queue.md — builds milestone gap report
2. C-suite (CPO + CTO + CFO + CLO + CRO) discusses: which milestone next, plan, risks
3. Selects ONE milestone per session
4. CPO drafts FEATURE blocks → appended to cleared-queue.md as Status: draft
5. Features await /oms FEATURE-NNN for task elaboration

## Always-active agents

CPO (lead), CTO, CFO, CLO, CRO — always all five, no exceptions.
PM not in exec — PM gap report injected into CPO briefing as context only.

## Key files

Read: product-direction.ctx.md, cleared-queue.md
Write: cleared-queue.md (FEATURE drafts, Status: draft), ceo-decisions.ctx.md (strategic holds)

## What it doesn't do

- Never writes tasks — FEATUREs only (tasks come from /oms FEATURE-NNN)
- Never implements — that's /oms-work
- Never selects more than one milestone per session
